TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Darkborn Elves Faction.

Translations.Comment: Dark elves. Used 'Nati dall'Oscurità' to convey darkborn nature; parallel to Spanish 'Nacidos de la Oscuridad' while maintaining Italian elegance.
Spear infantry. 'Lancieri' emphasizes professional soldiers; 'Oscuri' shows dark elf identity. Martial and concise.
Elite heavy infantry. Used 'Guardia del Terrore' to convey fear-radiating menace, parallel to Spanish 'Guardia del Terror'.
Dark magic users. 'Streghe' is classic Italian for witches; 'Oscure' (feminine plural) shows dark elf affiliation. Evocative.
Crossbow troops. 'Balestrier' emphasizes professional soldiers; 'Oscuri' maintains faction identity. Deadly ranged troops.
Stealthy infiltrators. Feminine plural 'Ombre' with 'Oscure' evokes stealth and darkness; maintains assassin aesthetic.
Heavy siege weapon. 'Balista Oscura' maintains consistency with war machine terminology across factions.
Mounted beast cavalry. 'Cavalieri' (knights) with 'Rapaci' (raptors/predatory) emphasizes fast, deadly nature.
Dark elf cavalry. Direct and military; 'Cavalieri Oscuri' shows dark mounted warriors on dark steeds.
War chariot. Masculine adjective 'Oscuro' emphasizes martial purpose and dark elf craftsmanship.
Multi-headed monster. Standard Italian mythological term for hydra, maintaining beast's fearsome nature.
Flying monsters. Standard Italian term; mythological creatures in service of dark elves.
Fearsome dragon. Direct translation; classic and imposing. Simple adjective emphasizes color and menace.
Corrupted flying mount. Kept mythological term 'Pegaso' with 'Oscuro' (dark) to show corruption and noble status.
Dark elf leader. Emphasizes both noble status and dark nature; singular form shows individual authority.
Female dark magic leader. 'Sacerdotessa' conveys religious authority and magical power; feminine form appropriate.
Master of stealth. Direct and menacing; singular form emphasizes deadly hero unit status and dark elf expertise.
